formations_blocks: Netherlands appear to be in a possession-based attacking shape, with Van de Ven temporarily at right-back
Japan are active in a compact but proactive midfield setup, pressing and combining down the left
adjustments: Van de Ven temporarily playing right-back
Japan repeatedly overload the left side with Maeda, Kubo and Nakamura linking up
phases.summary: The opening phase is bright and open, with both teams looking to attack rather than settle into a cautious block. The Netherlands create the clearest chance through a long, winding move that frees Malen, while Japan look sharper in possession and repeatedly threaten down the left with Maeda, Kubo and Nakamura combining. A notable early wrinkle is Van de Ven appearing at right-back, which Japan immediately target through direct wing play.

formations_blocks: Japan defending compactly enough to force shots/crosses from the edge and wide areas
Netherlands enjoying a good spell with sustained attacking pressure
adjustments: Netherlands attacking more through their left side with Gakpo involved
Japan creating from the right via Doan, with Maeda dropping off to link play
Van de Ven pushing high from left-back to join attacks
Gakpo cutting inside to deliver from the left half-space
Dumfries positioning very deep/appropriately to block shooting lanes and attack the far post
key_matchups: Maeda vs the Dutch back line
Van de Ven vs Japan's defensive right flank
phases.summary: This phase is defined by a pronounced slow-slow-quick rhythm, with both sides finding most of their danger down the left and the Netherlands growing into their strongest spell of the half. Japan’s best moments came from quick combinations on the right and Maeda’s link play, but Dutch defensive positioning repeatedly smothered the final action, while the Oranje threatened through overlaps, cutbacks and set-piece pressure.

The match reaches half-time level at 0-0 between the Netherlands and Japan, with no tactical detail provided in the commentary beyond the scoreline. The brief also notes that neither side made any half-time changes, so there is no evidence here of a shift in shape, pressing scheme, or specific matchups.

formations_blocks: Japan defending with a compact back line/low block
Netherlands attacking through wide areas and crosses
adjustments: Netherlands increased pressure after the break
Gravenberch advancing down the right to deliver crosses
Summerville running in behind and attacking the byline
Japan responded enough to level the game after falling behind
key_matchups: Ueda between Van Dijk and Van de Ven
phases.summary: The phase begins as a tight, cautious contest with Japan threatening from direct balls into Ueda, while the Netherlands look to exploit wide areas and second balls. After the interval, the Dutch grow stronger, generating crosses and back-post danger, which leads to Van Dijk’s opener before Japan equalize shortly after. The match then swings again as the Netherlands’ right-sided pressure and runs in behind keep Japan under sustained stress.

This phase is dominated by late drama rather than identifiable tactical shifts, with the Netherlands rescuing a draw through Kamada’s 89th-minute goal to make it 2-2 against Japan. The other listed result is Germany’s 7-1 win over Curaçao at full time, but the text provides no formation or pressing details for either match.
